description: Technician script library. Every script is read-only unless 'destructive' is true. Destructive scripts dry-run by default and require an explicit force flag to act.

conventions.macos: bash 3.2+. Run: bash <script> [args]. Scripts needing root say so and degrade gracefully without it.
conventions.collections: Evidence/output bundles are written to TechKit/collections/ on the USB drive.

scripts.summary: One-page overview: OS, hardware, BIOS/serial, RAM slots, uptime, domain join, disk summary, pending reboot.

args.name: -OutFile
args.desc: also save report to a text file

scripts.title: System Report

scripts.summary: SMART status, volume health, free space, dirty bit, disk errors from the System event log.
scripts.title: Disk Health

scripts.summary: Top CPU and memory consumers (sampled), memory/pagefile pressure.
scripts.title: Resource Usage

scripts.summary: Run keys, startup folders, logon scheduled tasks, auto-services that are stopped, WMI startup inventory.
scripts.title: Startup Programs

scripts.summary: Layered check: adapters, IP/DHCP, gateway ping, internet by IP, DNS, HTTP/captive portal, proxy, Wi-Fi signal.
scripts.title: Network Diagnostics

scripts.summary: Targeted test to one host: DNS, ping, TCP port, optional traceroute.
scripts.title: Connectivity Test

scripts.summary: Groups recent System/Application errors by source; lists distinct errors; flags BSODs, app crashes, dirty shutdowns.
scripts.title: Event Log Summary

scripts.summary: Export System/Application(/Security if admin) .evtx plus systeminfo/ipconfig/hotfixes to the USB collections folder.
use_when: need to take evidence away for analysis

args.name: -Destination
args.desc: output folder; defaults to USB collections

scripts.title: Export Event Logs

scripts.summary: Build/UBR, recent hotfixes, WU agent history with failures flagged, WU services, pending reboot, free space.
scripts.title: Update Status
Repair Windows Update
scripts.summary: Resets Windows Update: stops wuauserv/BITS/cryptsvc/msiserver, renames SoftwareDistribution + catroot2, clears the BITS queue, restarts services, triggers a fresh scan. Optional DISM+SFC and deep reset.

args.name: -RepairImage
args.desc: also run DISM RestoreHealth + SFC (15-45 min)

args.name: -DeepReset
args.desc: also re-register WU/BITS DLLs + reset Winsock (reboot after)

scripts.summary: AV state, firewall profiles, BitLocker, UAC, local admins, RDP/WinRM, SMBv1, SecureBoot/TPM.
scripts.title: Security Check

scripts.summary: Local users with last logon and password age, who is logged on, profile folder sizes, Administrators members.
scripts.title: User Accounts

scripts.summary: Reclaim disk space by removing STALE USER PROFILES (works on domain-joined machines - enumerates Win32_UserProfile, not just local accounts). Inactive = profile LastUseTime older than N days; reports per-profile size + TOTAL freed. Dry-run unless -Force. NEVER touches loaded/in-use profiles, the current user, Administrators members, or system profiles. Orphaned profiles (deleted account) need -IncludeOrphaned. -Delete also removes the LOCAL account (domain/AD accounts are left intact); -SkipLocalAccounts leaves local accounts alone entirely. -All targets every non-protected profile, ignoring last-use (e.g. before reimaging), since LastUseTime is bumped by background/service activity and can make all profiles look recently used.
use_when: reclaim disk from departed users' profiles (domain or local)
clean up stale profiles on shared/lab machines
disk full from old user folders in C:\Users

args.name: -DaysInactive
args.desc: idle threshold in days (by profile LastUseTime)

args.name: -All
args.desc: target EVERY profile except protected (current user, admins, in-use, system, -Exclude); ignores last-use - for clearing/reimaging a machine

args.name: -IncludeOrphaned
args.desc: also remove profiles whose account no longer exists

args.name: -Delete
args.desc: also delete the LOCAL account (not domain accounts)

args.name: -SkipLocalAccounts
args.desc: leave LOCAL accounts entirely alone; only reclaim domain/orphaned profiles

scripts.title: Remove Inactive Users

scripts.summary: Largest files, first-level folder sizes, known hogs (WU cache, hiberfil, Windows.old, recycle bin).
use_when: what is using all the space
scripts.title: Find Large Files

scripts.summary: Clean temp folders, WER queue, optionally WU download cache and Recycle Bin. Dry-run unless -Force.
use_when: before feature update on a full disk
scripts.admin: optional (Windows temp / WU cache need it)
scripts.title: Clean Temp Files

scripts.summary: Registry-based app inventory (64/32-bit/per-user) with install dates. -SortByDate finds 'what changed last week'.
use_when: did something install right before the problem started
scripts.title: Installed Software
Remove Microsoft 365 (Deep)
scripts.summary: Deep uninstall of Microsoft 365/Office: supported uninstall, then removes leftover folders, registry keys, scheduled tasks, and the ClickToRun service a normal uninstall misses; clears cached sign-in/credentials/licensing so products re-authenticate. Never deletes .pst or user docs.

args.name: -ResetActivationOnly
args.desc: don't uninstall; just clear sign-in/licensing to force re-auth

scripts.summary: Spooler state, printers/ports/drivers, stuck jobs. -ClearQueue purges the queue (admin).
scripts.title: Printer Diagnostics

scripts.summary: Battery health: design vs full-charge capacity, cycle count; generates HTML report. Skips desktops.
scripts.title: Battery Health

scripts.summary: DISM + SFC. Default diagnoses only; -Repair runs RestoreHealth + scannow (15-60 min).
use_when: missing DLL / corrupt system file errors
scripts.title: Repair System Files

scripts.summary: Runs all read-only diagnostics + raw extras (ipconfig, netstat, drivers, tasks), saves+zips to USB collections.
use_when: gather evidence for the AI to analyze
scripts.title: Full Triage Snapshot

scripts.summary: Model/chip/serial, macOS version, Rosetta, uptime, memory+swap pressure, storage summary, battery, reboot history.
scripts.title: System Report

scripts.summary: SMART status, APFS container usage, snapshot count; --verify runs live read-only volume check.

args.name: --verify
args.desc: live filesystem verify, a few minutes

scripts.title: Disk Health

scripts.summary: Top CPU/memory processes, memory pressure, swap, hung (I/O-stuck) processes, thermal throttling.
scripts.title: Resource Usage

scripts.summary: Third-party LaunchDaemons/Agents with target binaries, login items, BTM dump, running non-Apple jobs, cron.
scripts.title: Startup Programs

scripts.summary: Layered check: interfaces, gateway, internet by IP, DNS, captive-portal/HTTP, DNS config, proxy, Wi-Fi, VPN tunnels.
scripts.title: Network Diagnostics

scripts.summary: Targeted host test: DNS (system + direct), ping, TCP port via nc, optional traceroute.
scripts.title: Connectivity Test

scripts.summary: Crash/hang/panic reports (7d) with per-app frequency, unified-log errors by source, shutdown causes.
scripts.title: Crash & Log Summary

scripts.summary: Bundle crash reports + unified-log errors + install/system/wifi log tails into tar.gz on USB; sudo adds .logarchive.
use_when: gather data for the AI to analyze
scripts.title: Collect Logs

scripts.summary: Version/build, update history, auto-update settings; --check queries Apple for pending updates.
use_when: is this Mac up to date
scripts.title: Update Status

scripts.summary: SIP, Gatekeeper, FileVault, firewall, XProtect versions, admin users, SSH/screen/file sharing, 3rd-party extensions.
scripts.title: Security Check

scripts.summary: Local users (uid>=500) with admin flag and last login, logged-in users, home sizes (--sizes), hidden/disabled markers.
scripts.title: User Accounts

scripts.summary: Disable (default) or delete local users with no login in N days. Dry-run unless --force (sudo). Admin accounts are NEVER touched (no override). Also protects the current user.
scripts.title: Remove Inactive Users

scripts.summary: Largest files (Spotlight-accelerated), folder sizes, known hogs (iOS backups, Xcode, Docker, Trash, snapshots).
scripts.title: Find Large Files

scripts.summary: Clean user caches, 30d+ old logs, saved app state, optional Trash. Dry-run unless --force.
scripts.title: Clean Caches

scripts.summary: Cycle count, condition, capacity vs design from system_profiler + ioreg, charger info. Skips desktops.
scripts.title: Battery Health

scripts.summary: Runs all read-only diagnostics + raw extras (ifconfig, ps, system_profiler), saves+tars to USB collections.
scripts.title: Full Triage Snapshot
Remove Microsoft 365 (Deep)
scripts.summary: Deep uninstall of Microsoft 365/Office for macOS: quits apps, removes app bundles + ~/Library containers/prefs/caches, forgets pkg receipts, clears keychain identity + license so products re-authenticate. Preserves Outlook mail and never touches user docs.
use_when: office uninstall left files behind on mac

args.name: --remove-outlook-data
args.desc: also wipe local Outlook mail (re-syncs for M365)

scripts.title: Installed Software
scripts.summary: Inventory installed software: /Applications with versions, Homebrew formulae/casks, and Mac App Store apps. --sort-date finds what changed recently.
use_when: did something install right before the problem

scripts.title: Remove Adobe Creative Cloud (Deep)
scripts.summary: Deep uninstall of Adobe Creative Cloud + apps: stops Adobe processes/services (Genuine, Core Sync, AGS/AGM), runs supported uninstallers, removes leftover folders/registry/tasks, clears licensing (SLStore/SLCache) + sign-in (OOBE) so it re-authenticates. Keeps free Acrobat Reader; never touches creative files.
use_when: adobe genuine / core sync running after uninstall

args.name: -CleanHosts
args.desc: remove Adobe license-blocking hosts entries (backs up first)

scripts.title: Remove Adobe Creative Cloud (Deep)
scripts.summary: Deep uninstall of Adobe Creative Cloud + apps for macOS: quits processes, removes app bundles + launch daemons/agents + ~/Library and /Library Adobe leftovers, forgets pkg receipts, clears licensing (SLStore/SLCache) + sign-in (OOBE) + keychain so it re-authenticates. Keeps Acrobat Reader; never touches creative files.
use_when: adobe uninstall left files behind on mac
adobe genuine / core sync daemons after uninstall

scripts.title: Repair SCCM Client
scripts.summary: Refresh/repair the SCCM (ConfigMgr) client: trigger policy + inventory actions, clear ccmcache, restart CcmExec; optional ccmrepair + WMI repository salvage.

scripts.title: Reset Jamf Check-in
scripts.summary: Force a Jamf check-in/inventory/policy refresh (jamf policy/recon/manage + flush stuck policy history). Gated --reenroll removes the framework (unmanages device).

scripts.title: Reset OneDrive
scripts.summary: Reset OneDrive (onedrive /reset) to fix stuck/failed sync, then relaunch. Files are not deleted; they re-sync.

scripts.title: Reset OneDrive
scripts.summary: Reset OneDrive on macOS to fix stuck/failed sync, then relaunch. Files are not deleted; they re-sync.

scripts.title: Clear Teams Cache
scripts.summary: Clear Microsoft Teams cache (classic + new Teams) to fix freezes, blank screens, stale presence, login loops. Nothing of yours is lost.

scripts.title: Clear Teams Cache
scripts.summary: Clear Microsoft Teams cache on macOS (classic + new Teams) to fix freezes, blank screens, login loops.

scripts.title: Repair Network
scripts.summary: One-click network repair: flush DNS, reset Winsock/IP stack, renew DHCP, clear ARP, restart adapter. Stack reset needs a reboot.

scripts.title: Repair Network
scripts.summary: One-click network repair for macOS: flush DNS, renew DHCP, toggle Wi-Fi, clear ARP.
BitLocker status for every volume, key protectors, and the recovery password + key ID (elevated). Reports TPM readiness.
use_when: before a reimage or board swap
user locked out / needs recovery key

args.name: -OutFile
args.desc: also save the report (incl. recovery key) to a file

scripts.title: Encryption & Recovery Key

scripts.summary: FileVault status, enabled unlock users, recovery-key posture (personal/institutional), and APFS volume encryption.
scripts.title: Encryption & Recovery Key

scripts.summary: Stop the spooler, clear stuck jobs in spool\PRINTERS, and restart it. Dry-run unless -Force; -RestartOnly keeps jobs.
scripts.title: Reset Print Spooler

scripts.summary: Clear stuck print jobs and restart CUPS. Dry-run unless --force; --remove-printers does the full reset.
scripts.title: Reset Printing System

scripts.summary: AD secure channel, domain controller, Kerberos tickets, time sync, and applied GPOs. -Repair forces gpupdate + time resync.
use_when: can't log in to a domain account
GPO or mapped drives not applying
scripts.title: Domain & Group Policy

scripts.summary: AD binding, Kerberos tickets, and time sync (clock skew breaks SSO). --repair renews Kerberos and resyncs time.
scripts.title: Domain & Directory

scripts.summary: Live link quality (signal, channel, band, BSSID, rates), adapter driver, and saved profiles. -Report saves the full WLAN report.

args.name: -Report
args.desc: save full WLAN report (HTML) to collections

scripts.title: Wi-Fi Diagnostics

scripts.summary: Live link quality (RSSI/noise, channel, PHY mode, security, rate), radio/driver info, and saved networks.
scripts.title: Wi-Fi Diagnostics

scripts.summary: Detects pending-reboot flags (CBS, Windows Update, file renames, rename, SCCM) and true uptime; warns when Fast Startup hides reboots.
scripts.title: Pending Reboot & Uptime

scripts.summary: Uptime and reboot history, plus pending macOS updates and staged installers that need a restart.
scripts.title: Pending Reboot & Uptime

scripts.summary: Blue-screen bugcheck codes, unexpected shutdowns (Kernel-Power 41 / 6008), top app crashes, and minidump files on disk.
scripts.title: Crash & BSOD Report

scripts.summary: Kernel panics, real app crashes (memory-pressure kills shown separately), top crashing apps, and previous shutdown causes.
scripts.title: Crash & Panic Report

scripts.summary: Preview the size of a user's Desktop/Documents/Downloads/etc; with -Force -Destination copies them via robocopy. Source untouched.
use_when: how big is the user's data
scripts.title: Backup User Data

scripts.summary: Preview the size of a user's key folders; with --force --dest copies them via rsync. Source untouched.
use_when: how big is the user's data
scripts.title: Backup User Data

scripts.summary: Defender status, signature age, threat history, and on-demand scan. -Force scans (quick or -Full), -Update refreshes signatures.
scripts.title: Antivirus Scan

scripts.summary: Defender (mdatp) status, threats and on-demand scan if installed; otherwise built-in XProtect/Gatekeeper status and recent quarantined downloads.
scripts.title: Antivirus Scan

scripts.summary: Run keys, startup folders, non-Microsoft scheduled tasks, auto services in odd paths, and WMI subscriptions; suspicious entries flagged.
scripts.title: Startup & Persistence Audit

scripts.summary: Third-party launch agents/daemons, login items, cron, and configuration profiles; suspicious paths flagged.
scripts.title: Startup & Persistence Audit
